member.name: Zöe Franklin
member.synopsis: Zöe Franklin is the Liberal Democrat MP for Guildford , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Local Government).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Breast Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of (a) lowering the minimum age for routine breast screening to 40 and (b) offering annual screening on (i) early detection rates, (ii) patient o
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are guided by the independent scientific advice of the UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC) on all screening matters. It is only where there is robust evidence that an offer to screen provides more good than harm that a screening programme is recom...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Gratuities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he plans to introduce legislation to prohibit the automatic addition of service charges to customer bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers (DMCCA) Act 2024 requires traders to display prices inclusive of all taxes and unavoidable charges before payment is made. Prices must be accurate and not misleading. Failure to do this may be taken as an unf...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorcycles: Delivery Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the use of provisional licence holders riding mopeds displaying learner plates (L-plates) to undertake commercial delivery work; and whether she plans to introduce measures to pr
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government expects those driving or riding for work to keep both themselves and other road users safe. As such we have committed to piloting a new voluntary Work-Related Road Safety Charter that is being developed in partnership with industry. The Cha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Plastics: Health Hazards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he plans to review evidence on chemical exposure from plastic baby products, including feeding bottles, dummies and teethers; and if he will make an assessment of the adequacy of the regulatory
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes the safety of baby products seriously and has robust regulations in place requiring that all products placed on the UK market are safe. Specific rules exist under product safety and chemicals regulations, to address chemical exposure ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Transfers: Sexual Offences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of prisoner transfer arrangements for serious sexual offenders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to deporting foreign national offenders through every means available, including Prisoner Transfers. In the year ending March 2026 deportations of FNOs, including murderers and rapists, were up 13% compared to the previous yea...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Music: Ethnic Groups
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the implications for her policies of the findings of UK Music's recent Black Music Means Business report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We welcome the publication of UK Music's landmark Black Music Means Business report, which highlights that Black music is a central commercial engine of the sector but rightly notes that equity and career progression have not kept pace. We will soon launc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Ataxia: Diagnosis and Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve early and equitable access to diagnosis and treatment for people living with ataxia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving the lives of those living with rare diseases, including rare ataxias, under the UK Rare Diseases Framework. We published the fifth annual England action plan in February 2026, where we report on the steps we have t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Fractures: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to publish the implementation plan for delivering the Government's commitment to provide universal access to Fracture Liaison Services across England by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our 10-Year Health Plan committed to rolling out Fracture Liaison Services across every part of the country by 2030.Integrated care boards (ICBs) remain well placed to make decisions according to local need.The Renewed Women’s Health Strategy sets an expe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the reasons for the continued difference in survivor pension entitlements between the Police Pension Scheme 1987 and the 2006 and 2015 Police Pension Schemes in relatio
writtenQuestions.answerText: Serving police officers have access to the 2015 police pension scheme, which provides life-long survivor benefits for spouses, civil partners and unmarried partners, including those who remarry or cohabit after losing a spouse. All eligible police officer...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Councillors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the current statutory framework for parish councillors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government believes that the current statutory framework for parish councillors is proportionate and adequate. When parliamentary time allows, the Government intends to introduce legislation for a range of measures to create a clearer and consistently...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Circumcision
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what consideration he has given to (a) introducing safeguards and (b) reviewing legislation relating to the circumcision of infants and minors for non-medical reasons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Departmental officials will be engaging with stakeholders, particularly in Muslim and Jewish communities, regarding non-statutory measures to improve patient safety relating to the circumcision of male infants and minors for non-medical reasons.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Councillors: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a framework to enable parish councils to remunerate elected parish councillors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parish and town councils may already pay a basic allowance to their councillors. It is up to individual councils to set levels of allowances appropriate for their individual circumstances.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Councillors: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the absence of remuneration for parish councillors on the recruitment and retention of those councillors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parish and town councils may already pay a basic allowance to their councillors. It is up to individual councils to set levels of allowances appropriate for their individual circumstances.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what support her Department is providing to local education authorities with place planning following recent trends in birth rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local authorities have a statutory responsibility to ensure there are enough school places available in their area for every child of compulsory school age. We expect local authorities, academy trusts and local partners to engage collaboratively and const...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will take steps, including through the UK’s role in the G20, to support reform of the international sovereign debt system to help ensure timely and effective debt relief for countries in financial crisis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. member to the answers given to UIN3600 on the 2nd of June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she is taking steps to bring forward legislative proposals to require private creditors to participate in sovereign debt relief arrangements on comparable terms to official lenders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. member to the answers given to UIN107484 on the 28th of January 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
